paper.title: Discovery of Galactic center ejected star in DESI DR1

extraction.summary: Extracted one HVS candidate DESI-312 (Gaia DR3 4226880901740499200) introduced by this paper. The paper explicitly presents it as a strong Galactic Center ejecta candidate with a trajectory traceable to within 2 kpc of Sgr A*.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR3 astrometry: positions, proper motions, parallaxes, and RUWE.

source_refs.context: Gaia DR3 astrometry combined with DESI

method_chain.summary: DESI DR1 stellar catalogue (MWS): spectra, radial velocities, and stellar parameters from RVS pipeline.

source_refs.context: DESI stellar catalogue with RVS pipeline measurements

method_chain.summary: Photometric measurements from Gaia, SDSS, and 2MASS.

source_refs.context: Photometric observations from Gaia, SDSS, SkyMapper, and 2MASS

method_chain.summary: Specdis spectrophotometric distance catalogue providing distances for MWS stars.
Cross-match of DESI stellar catalogue with Gaia DR3 using the crossmatch included in the DESI stellar catalogue.

source_refs.context: Gaia crossmatch included in DESI stellar catalogue
Table caption: Parallax corresponds to the zero-point corrected value

method_chain.summary: Heliocentric distances from Specdis neural network and Gaia parallax.
Radial velocities from DESI RVS pipeline (rvspecfit).
Stellar parameters (Teff, logg, FeH) from DESI RVS pipeline (rvspecfit) with corrected metallicity calibration.

source_refs.context: Stellar parameters from RVS pipeline with corrected metallicity

method_chain.summary: Transformation to Galactocentric reference frame using Astropy, assuming solar position and velocity. Selection of |V_rf| > 300 km/s.
Price-Whelan 2017 MilkyWayPotential2022 ported to AGAMA: nucleus, bulge, three Miyamoto-Nagai discs, NFW halo.
Backward orbit integration over 0.5 Gyr with 1000 Monte Carlo samples, recording Galactic plane crossings.
Computation of ratio_gc and ejection velocities from plane crossing positions within 1 kpc of the GC.

source_refs.context: Ejection velocity spectrum from orbit realizations

method_chain.summary: Final candidate selection: ratio_gc >= 0.5 and supersolar metallicity (FeH > 0) to suppress GSE contamination.

source_refs.context: Candidate selection criteria including metallicity cut

method_chain.summary: MIST isochrone fitting with minimint to photometry from Gaia, SDSS, 2MASS, with spectroscopic constraints on Teff and logg.
outputs: Stellar mass, age, distance, extinction posteriors

method_chain.summary: Adoption of reported spectroscopic observables (SNR, vsini) from DESI data release.

source_refs.context: Reported signal-to-noise ratios from DESI observations

method_chain.summary: Adoption of reported Galactocentric position quantities (distance from GC, height below plane) from the paper text.

source_refs.context: Kinematic properties summary from paper text

inclusion_assessment.summary: The paper introduces DESI-312 as a strong HVS candidate ejected from the Galactic Center. Its trajectory can be confidently traced back to within 2 kpc of Sgr A*. The paper explicitly includes GC ejecta regardless of escape status ('we do not exclude bound objects') and discusses DESI-312 as a possible GC ejecta via the Hills mechanism. Alternative origins (YMC, disk, globular cluster, LMC, Sagittarius Dwarf) are considered and rejected or found unlikely.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly and repeatedly identifies DESI-312 as the single HVS candidate from their 6D search, with dedicated sections discussing its candidate status, kinematics, and origin. The paper's search methodology explicitly admits bound objects and treats DESI-312 as a compelling GC ejecta candidate.

source_refs.context: Selection admits both bound and unbound ejecta from the GC
We do not exclude bound objects
Paper introduces the candidate for the first time

candidate_groups_considered.description: Single HVS candidate DESI-312 identified from the DESI DR1 6D search combining kinematics, orbit integration, and supersolar metallicity.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly presents DESI-312 as a strong HVS candidate ejected from the Galactic Center. It is the sole surviving object after applying the full selection pipeline (kinematic |V_rf|>300 km/s, ratio_gc>=0.5, FeH>0). The paper explicitly includes GC ejecta regardless of whether they have sufficient energy to escape the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: We do not exclude objects that remain gravitationally attached to the Galaxy
Dedicated subsection for HVS candidate DESI-312
